subject: English
note: Achievement standards from the Australian Curriculum Version 9 English. Three strands: Language, Literature, Literacy. By individual year level. Verify at australiancurriculum.edu.au.

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Listen to and read aloud short, predictable texts with familiar vocabulary; demonstrate concepts of print (left-right, top-bottom); write simple words and sentences using known letters

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Read decodable and predictable texts with increasing fluency; use knowledge of phonics and sight words; create short texts for known purposes using simple language features

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Read a range of texts with fluency and comprehension; use comprehension strategies (predicting, questioning, monitoring); create informative, imaginative, and persuasive texts with basic structure

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Discuss and compare texts; identify how language choices create meaning and effect; explore how texts reflect social and cultural contexts

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Plan, draft, and publish imaginative, informative, and persuasive texts; use appropriate punctuation, vocabulary, and sentence structures for audience and purpose

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Analyse how text structures, language features, and visual elements work together; compare responses to texts from different cultures and perspectives

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Explain how authors use language, structure, and visual choices to influence the reader; identify themes and social, moral, and cultural contexts of texts

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Produce well-structured texts for a range of purposes and audiences; use complex sentences, paragraphing, and a variety of language features effectively

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Analyse how texts are shaped by purpose, audience, and context; compare and contrast the use of language and structure in different text types

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Analyse how authors develop character, setting, and themes; evaluate the impact of language choices; discuss multiple perspectives and interpretations

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Produce texts for different purposes and audiences using sophisticated language features; develop and sustain an argument; review and edit for clarity and effect

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Analyse how language, structure, and visual features construct meaning and influence the reader; evaluate the effectiveness of texts for their purpose and context

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Interpret and evaluate texts by analysing how language, form, and context shape meaning and represent ideas; compare perspectives across texts

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Create sustained and complex texts for specific purposes and audiences; demonstrate control of grammar, vocabulary, and writing conventions; construct and develop a reasoned argument

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Critically evaluate texts by analysing how language, structure, and context shape meaning; synthesise ideas from multiple texts; assess the reliability and validity of sources

objectives.subject: English
objectives.text: Create sophisticated texts that reflect complex ideas and sustained argument; use language with deliberate and precise effect for a specific audience and purpose; self-edit and refine writing
